title: Senate advances resolution to end Iran war 50-47 — GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy flips after primary loss, joins Paul/Collins/Murkowski as 4th Republican
snippet: The U.S. Senate voted 50-47 to advance a war powers resolution aimed at ending U.S. military involvement in the Iran war. Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-La.) became the fourth Republican to support the measure, joining Rand Paul, Susan Collins, and Lisa Murkowski, after losing his primary race. The resolution, co-sponsored by Sens. Tim Kaine (D-Va.) and Rand Paul (R-Ky.), invokes the War Powers Resolution to withdraw U.S. forces from hostilities with Iran within 30 days. The measure still needs a full Senate vote, a House vote, and faces a likely presidential veto. Cassidy said 'the American people deserve a debate and a vote on whether we should be at war' after his primary loss freed him from party pressure.
tag_reason: 신규 — 상원 전쟁권한결의안이 50-47로 처음 통과한 전환적 사건. 캐시디 이탈로 공화당 4번째 반대표 확보. 이전 보도(5/14 src-1090 등)에서 추적해온 의회 반전 움직임의 새로운 이정표

entities.name: Bill Cassidy
entities.role: GOP Senator, Louisiana, flipped on WPR vote
